name: Carissa Forest Num-Num Root
description: A thorny evergreen indigenous shrub. Root extracts have a rich traditional history of treating chronic chest pains, severe joint and neck stiffness, and intestinal hookworm infections.
preparation: Root decoction: Boil 5g of clean dried sliced roots in 500ml of water for 25 minutes. Filter and administer 50ml warm once daily after food.
safetyAlert: Roots contain trace cardiac glycosides. Strictly adhere to standard dosage limits; avoid chronic administration exceeding 14 days.
interactions: None reported in traditional literature. Do not combine with modern digitalis or heart rate slowing drugs.

demographicCautions.title: ABSOLUTE CONTRAINDICATION in Infants
demographicCautions.message: Due to trace cardiac glycoside active constituents, do not administer Carissa spinarum root decoctions to infants or children under 5 years old.
